name: Cleveland Selfreliance
description_short: Cleveland Selfreliance is a not-for-profit federal credit union serving the Ukrainian-American community for 60+ years, offering mortgages, auto loans, savings accounts, and online banking.
description_long:
Cleveland Selfreliance Federal Credit Union was established to serve the Ukrainian-American community and has operated for over 60 years. Based in Cleveland, Ohio with additional branches in Lorain and Canton, the institution operates as a member-owned, not-for-profit credit union federally insured by the NCUA with deposits protected up to $250,000 per member.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including mortgage lending (15 and 30-year conventional mortgages), auto loans, personal loans, home equity lines of credit (up to $200,000), VISA credit cards, and savings products such as regular shares, certificates of deposit, and IRAs. They provide digital banking capabilities through online banking portals, direct deposit services, ACH payroll deposits, safe deposit boxes, money orders, and MAC ATM access. Current promotional rates include 5.00% for 30-year mortgages (new purchase only, $1M max), 4.75% for home equity lines of credit and new auto loans (2024-2026), and 5.49% for 15-year mortgages.

What distinguishes Cleveland Selfreliance is their deep community roots within the Ukrainian-American population, bilingual services (English and Ukrainian), and their explicit organizational commitment to supporting Ukrainian independence and freedom. As a not-for-profit institution, they return earnings to members through lower loan rates and higher savings rates rather than generating shareholder profits. The credit union actively maintains community connections through member meetings and charitable outreach.

While Cleveland Selfreliance offers competitive rates and genuine member-ownership benefits, potential members should note that as a specialized community credit union, membership eligibility may be limited to those with Ukrainian heritage or community ties. Limited branch availability (three locations) may restrict convenient access for those outside the Cleveland-Lorain-Canton corridor. The organization's small-to-mid-size profile means fewer digital tools and services compared to national banks.
services: 30-year conventional mortgages (new purchase, up to $1M at 5.00%)
15-year conventional mortgages (new purchase or refinance at 5.49%)
Home equity lines of credit (up to $200,000 at 4.75%)
Direct deposit of Social Security and federal payments
Online banking with home banking portal
pros: Not-for-profit structure returns earnings to members as lower loan rates and higher savings rates
Competitive mortgage rates (5.00% for 30-year new purchase mortgages, 5.49% for 15-year)
Home equity lines of credit available up to $200,000 at 4.75%
NCUA federal insurance protects member deposits up to $250,000
Bilingual services in English and Ukrainian for community accessibility
Member-owned governance means customers have voting ownership rights
Offers free life insurance up to $2,000 to qualifying members
Online banking and digital services available; accepts online mortgage applications
cons: Limited branch network (only 3 locations: Cleveland, Lorain, Canton) restricts physical access
Membership may be restricted to Ukrainian-American community or those with qualifying ties, limiting eligibility
Limited hours of operation (closed Wednesdays, Monday-Tuesday 9AM-3PM only)
Smaller institution likely means fewer digital tools, mobile app features, and investment options compared to large national credit unions
Mortgage products limited to new purchases with $1M cap on 30-year conventional mortgages
best_for: Ukrainian-American families and community members seeking culturally-aligned banking with bilingual support
Homebuyers in the Cleveland/Northeast Ohio area looking for competitive mortgage rates with community roots
Credit union members prioritizing not-for-profit structures and member ownership over corporate banking
Borrowers seeking home equity lines of credit up to $200,000 with competitive rates
diagnosis: Cleveland Selfreliance is best for Ukrainian-American community members and homebuyers in Northeast Ohio seeking competitive rates through a member-owned, not-for-profit credit union with bilingual services and community commitment. Primary caveat: membership eligibility may be restricted to Ukrainian-heritage individuals or community ties, and the three-location footprint limits convenient branch access outside Cleveland, Lorain, and Canton areas.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
